(Son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}}, Prince of Emperor {{ill|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}) | clan_patriarch = | type = {{ill|Imperial clans of Japan|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja|近江国坂田郡}} | notable_members = | descendants = }} [[File:Yamatsuteru-shrine.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山津照神社}}]] [[File:Okinaga-postoffice.jpg|thumb|300px|Okinaga Post Office, preserving the Okinaga name.]] '''Okinaga Clan''' (息長氏, Okinaga-uji) is a clan named after the "Okinaga" title. == Overview == The first mention of "Okinaga" in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} appears with {{ill|Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja|息長水依比賣}}, the daughter of {{ill|Ame no Mikage no Kami|ja|天目一箇神}}’s sixth descendant, {{ill|Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja|国忍富命}}, and the wife of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of Emperor {{ill|Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. Subsequent mentions include: 1. {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}}, a son of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}} and father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, also referred to as "Kinaga Sukune no Mikoto" in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. 2. {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, the daughter of Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto and the wife of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant burial mounds ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[ja:息長氏]]

According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant burial mounds ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[ja:息長氏]]

[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans (Kofun Period) | clan_name = Okinaga Clan | image = | family_name = Okinaga [[Mahito]] | founder = | origin = {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}
(Son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}}, Prince of Emperor {{ill|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}) | clan_patriarch = | type = {{ill|Imperial clans of Japan|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja|近江国坂田郡}} | notable_members = | descendants = }} [[File:Yamatsuteru-shrine.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山津照神社}}]] [[File:Okinaga-postoffice.jpg|thumb|300px|Okinaga Post Office, preserving the Okinaga name.]] '''Okinaga Clan''' (息長氏, Okinaga-uji) is a clan named after the "Okinaga" title. == Overview == The first mention of "Okinaga" in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} appears with {{ill|Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja|息長水依比賣}}, the daughter of {{ill|Ame no Mikage no Kami|ja|天目一箇神}}’s sixth descendant, {{ill|Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja|国忍富命}}, and the wife of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of Emperor {{ill|Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. Subsequent mentions include: 1. {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}}, a son of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}} and father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, also referred to as "Kinaga Sukune no Mikoto" in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. 2. {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, the daughter of Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto and the wife of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant burial mounds ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[ja:息長氏]]

[[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Okinaga Clan-qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Okinaga Clan-qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans (Kofun Period) | clan_name = Okinaga Clan | image = | family_name = Okinaga [[Mahito]] | founder = | origin = {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}
(Son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}}, Prince of Emperor {{ill|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}) | clan_patriarch = | type = {{ill|Imperial clans of Japan|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja|近江国坂田郡}} | notable_members = | descendants = }} [[File:Yamatsuteru-shrine.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山津照神社}}]] [[File:Okinaga-postoffice.jpg|thumb|300px|Okinaga Post Office, preserving the Okinaga name.]] '''Okinaga Clan''' (息長氏, Okinaga-uji) is a clan named after the "Okinaga" title. == Overview == The first mention of "Okinaga" in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} appears with {{ill|Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja|息長水依比賣}}, the daughter of {{ill|Ame no Mikage no Kami|ja|天目一箇神}}’s sixth descendant, {{ill|Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja|国忍富命}}, and the wife of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of Emperor {{ill|Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. Subsequent mentions include: 1. {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}}, a son of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}} and father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, also referred to as "Kinaga Sukune no Mikoto" in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. 2. {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, the daughter of Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto and the wife of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant burial mounds ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Okinaga Clan-qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Okinaga Clan-qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[ja:息長氏]]

{{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = 息長氏 |画像 = |氏姓 = 息長[[真人]] |始祖 = |出自 = [[意富富杼王]]
（第15代[[応神天皇]]の[[皇子]][[若野毛二俣王]]の[[王子]]） |氏祖 = |種別 = [[皇別]] |本貫 = [[近江国]]坂田郡 |人物 = |後裔 = }} [[File:Yamatsuteru-shrine.jpg|thumb|300px|[[山津照神社]]]] [[File:Okinaga-postoffice.jpg|thumb|300px|息長氏の名残を残す息長郵便局]] '''息長氏'''（おきながうじ）は、「息長」を[[氏]]の名とする[[氏族]]である。 == 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』における「息長」の文字の初出は、[[息長水依比賣]]である。父は[[天目一箇神|天之御影神]]の六世孫・[[国忍富命]]、夫は[[日子坐王]]（第9代[[開化天皇]]の子）とされる。次は、[[開化天皇]]皇子の[[日子坐王]]の子である[[迦邇米雷王]]を父とする[[息長宿禰王]]（『[[日本書紀|紀]]』では気長宿禰王）であり、[[神功皇后]]の[[父]]とされる。第三は、息長宿禰王の娘で第14代[[仲哀天皇]]の[[皇后]]・[[神功皇后]]（じんぐうこうごう）であり、諱（実名）は『古事記』では[[息長帯比売命]]（おきながたらしひめのみこと）、『日本書紀』では気長足姫尊とされる。第四は、神功皇后の弟の[[息長日子王]]、第五は、[[倭建命]]の[[子]]である[[息長田別王]]、第六は、倭建命の子・[[杙俣長日子王]]の娘である[[息長真若中比売]]。その他、[[香春神社]]の祭神である[[香春神社|辛国息長大姫刀自命]]や、『[[続日本紀]]』に見える額田部連息長、[[吉備津彦]]後裔の吉備上道臣息長借鎌、『[[播磨国風土記]]』に見える[[天穂日命]]の後裔で山直の祖・息長命、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』で茨田連の祖と見える[[景行天皇]]皇子の息長彦人大兄瑞城命などがいる。 『[[記紀]]』によると[[応神天皇]]の[[皇子]][[若野毛二俣王]]の子、[[意富富杼王]]が中興の祖とされる{{Sfn|宝賀|2014|p=8}}。[[皇室]]との関わりを語る説話が多く、[[カバネ|姓]]（かばね）は[[キミ (カバネ)|公]]（または君、きみ）であり、同族に[[三国氏|三国公]]（後の三国真人）・[[坂田氏|坂田公]]（後の坂田真人）・[[酒人氏|酒人公]]（後の酒人真人）・[[波多氏 (古代)#皇別氏族（息長氏族）|波多君]]（後の八多真人）など、[[準皇族]]のニュアンスのある公や[[真人]]の姓（かばね）である。 [[岡田精司]]は『[[継体天皇]]＝息長出自説が有力な継体王朝論』とする{{Sfn|大橋|1984|p=122}}。『[[古事記]]』において[[継体天皇]]の出身地を[[近江国|近江]]とする記述については、『応神天皇の子孫である袁本杼命を近江から迎え入れる、』{{Sfn|竹田|2016|p=7-8}}と記載が有り、中村啓信の新版古事記現代語訳付きにも同様の記載が有る{{Sfn|中村|2015|p=453}}。 [[奈良時代]]以降は、[[近江国]]坂田郡（現在の[[滋賀県]][[米原市]]のほとんどと[[長浜市]]の一部）を本拠としたとするのが一般的な見解とされており{{Sfn|宝賀|2014|p=7}}、[[美濃国|美濃]]・[[越国|越]]への交通の要地であり、[[天野川 (滋賀県)|天野川]]河口にある朝妻津により大津・[[琵琶湖]]北岸の塩津とも繋がる。また、[[村居田古墳|息長古墳群]]を擁し相当の力をもった[[豪族]]であったことが窺える。ただし文献的に記述が少なく、謎の氏族ともいわれる。一方、[[河内国|河内]]に息長氏末裔が近世まで存在しており、文献などには信頼性が欠ける部分も多いが、看過出来ない部分もあり、河内が本拠であるという説もある{{要出典|date=2022年6月}}。 [[7世紀]]末に[[天武天皇]]は、豪族を新たに八種にランクづける[[八色の姓]]を制定した。息長氏はその最高位の「[[真人]]」を賜った。 なお、[[山津照神社]]は息長氏の祖神を祀ったとされ、現在は[[国之常立神|国常立命]]を祭神とする。 == 椿井文書と息長氏 == [[椿井政隆]]は、自身が作成した[[偽書]]・「[[椿井文書]]」において、現在の[[米原市]]で[[朝妻川]]と呼ばれていた川に「'''息長川'''」の名称を与え、「[[朝嬬皇女]]墳」を世継村に、「[[星川稚宮皇子]]墳」を朝妻川の対岸の朝妻村に「設置」した。朝妻川は天の川とも呼ばれていたので、椿井は[[七夕伝説]]を作り出そうとしたのであり、湖北の七夕伝承は椿井文書由来のものである馬部隆弘「椿井文書―日本最大級の偽文書」（中央公論新社、2020年）。 また、椿井は[[京都府]][[京田辺市]]の[[観音寺 (京田辺市)|観音寺]]を「中世までは普賢寺あるいは普賢教法寺と称していた」ことにし、また「[[朱智神社]]をその鎮守である」とし、さらに寺に「'''息長山'''」の山号を与えた。そして、「息長」や「朱智」という苗字の侍を祖とする系図を量産した。ここに息長という名詞が登場するのは、椿井政隆が若い頃に[[近江国]][[膳所藩]]で活動しており、その時に得た息長氏の知識を踏まえているからである。これは、逆に「南山城に息長氏が存在したという伝承が存在しなかった」ことを表している。 date = 1984-3-10 | isbn = 4-642-02153-1 | ref = {{Sfnref|大橋|1984}} }} * {{Cite book | 和書 | author=竹田恒泰|authorlink=竹田恒泰 | title = 現代語古事記　ポケット版 | series = | publisher = 学研プラス | volume = | edition = 1st | date = 2016-6-28 | isbn = 978-4-05-406454-6 | ref = {{Sfnref|竹田|2016}} }} * {{Cite book | 和書 | author=中村啓信|authorlink=中村啓信 | title = 新版 古事記 現代語訳付き | series = 角川文庫 15906 | publisher = 株式会社KADOKAWA | volume = | edition = 11th | date = 2015-5-15 | isbn = 978-4-04-400104-9 | ref = {{Sfnref|中村|2015}} }} * {{Cite book | 和書 | author=宝賀寿男|authorlink=宝賀寿男 | title = 息長氏 ー大王を輩出した鍛冶氏族 | series = 古代氏族の研究(6) | publisher = 星雲社 | volume = | edition = 1st | date = 2014-11-5 | isbn = 978-4-434-19823-6 | ref = {{Sfnref|宝賀|2014}} }} == 関連項目 == * [[息長宿禰王]] * [[息長真手王]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:おきなかし}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Okinaga Clan-qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Okinaga Clan-qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans (Kofun Period) | clan_name = Okinaga Clan | image = | family_name = Okinaga [[Mahito]] | founder = | origin = {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}
(Son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}}, Prince of Emperor {{ill|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}) | clan_patriarch = | type = {{ill|Imperial clans of Japan|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja|近江国坂田郡}} | notable_members = | descendants = }} [[File:Yamatsuteru-shrine.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山津照神社}}]] [[File:Okinaga-postoffice.jpg|thumb|300px|Okinaga Post Office, preserving the Okinaga name.]] '''Okinaga Clan''' (息長氏, Okinaga-uji) is a clan named after the "Okinaga" title. == Overview == The first mention of "Okinaga" in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} appears with {{ill|Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja|息長水依比賣}}, the daughter of {{ill|Ame no Mikage no Kami|ja|天目一箇神}}’s sixth descendant, {{ill|Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja|国忍富命}}, and the wife of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of Emperor {{ill|Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. Subsequent mentions include: 1. {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}}, a son of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}} and father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, also referred to as "Kinaga Sukune no Mikoto" in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. 2. {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, the daughter of Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto and the wife of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant burial mounds ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Okinaga Clan-qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Okinaga Clan-qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:息長氏]]

(Son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}}, Prince of Emperor {{ill|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}) | clan_patriarch = | type = {{ill|Imperial clans of Japan|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja|近江国坂田郡}} | notable_members = | descendants = }} [[File:Yamatsuteru-shrine.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山津照神社}}]] [[File:Okinaga-postoffice.jpg|thumb|300px|Okinaga Post Office, preserving the Okinaga name.]] '''Okinaga Clan''' (息長氏, Okinaga-uji) is a clan named after the "Okinaga" title. == Overview == The first mention of "Okinaga" in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} appears with {{ill|Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja|息長水依比賣}}, the daughter of {{ill|Ame no Mikage no Kami|ja|天目一箇神}}’s sixth descendant, {{ill|Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja|国忍富命}}, and the wife of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of Emperor {{ill|Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. Subsequent mentions include: 1. {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}}, a son of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}} and father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, also referred to as "Kinaga Sukune no Mikoto" in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. 2. {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, the daughter of Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto and the wife of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant burial mounds ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Okinaga Clan-qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Okinaga Clan-qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]]

According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant burial mounds ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Okinaga Clan-qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Okinaga Clan-qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]]

[[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Okinaga Clan-qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Okinaga Clan-qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans (Kofun Period) | clan_name = Okinaga Clan | image = | family_name = Okinaga [[Mahito]] | founder = | origin = {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}
(Son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}}, Prince of Emperor {{ill|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}) | clan_patriarch = | type = {{ill|Imperial clans of Japan|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja|近江国坂田郡}} | notable_members = | descendants = }} [[File:Yamatsuteru-shrine.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山津照神社}}]] [[File:Okinaga-postoffice.jpg|thumb|300px|Okinaga Post Office, preserving the Okinaga name.]] '''Okinaga Clan''' (息長氏, Okinaga-uji) is a clan named after the "Okinaga" title. == Overview == The first mention of "Okinaga" in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} appears with {{ill|Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja|息長水依比賣}}, the daughter of {{ill|Ame no Mikage no Kami|ja|天目一箇神}}’s sixth descendant, {{ill|Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja|国忍富命}}, and the wife of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of Emperor {{ill|Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. Subsequent mentions include: 1. {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}}, a son of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}} and father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, also referred to as "Kinaga Sukune no Mikoto" in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. 2. {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, the daughter of Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto and the wife of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant burial mounds ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Okinaga Clan-qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Okinaga Clan-qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Okinaga Clan-qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Okinaga Clan-qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

[[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Okinaga Clan-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Okinaga Clan-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans (Kofun Period) | clan_name = Okinaga Clan | image = | family_name = Okinaga [[Mahito]] | founder = | origin = {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}
(Son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}}, Prince of Emperor {{ill|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}) | clan_patriarch = | type = {{ill|Imperial clans of Japan|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja|近江国坂田郡}} | notable_members = | descendants = }} [[File:Yamatsuteru-shrine.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山津照神社}}]] [[File:Okinaga-postoffice.jpg|thumb|300px|Okinaga Post Office, preserving the Okinaga name.]] '''Okinaga Clan''' (息長氏, Okinaga-uji) is a clan named after the "Okinaga" title. == Overview == The first mention of "Okinaga" in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} appears with {{ill|Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja|息長水依比賣}}, the daughter of {{ill|Ame no Mikage no Kami|ja|天目一箇神}}’s sixth descendant, {{ill|Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja|国忍富命}}, and the wife of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of Emperor {{ill|Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. Subsequent mentions include: 1. {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}}, a son of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}} and father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, also referred to as "Kinaga Sukune no Mikoto" in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. 2. {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, the daughter of Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto and the wife of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant burial mounds ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Okinaga Clan-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Okinaga Clan-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

[[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans (Kofun Period) | clan_name = Okinaga Clan | image = | family_name = Okinaga [[Mahito]] | founder = | origin = {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}
(Son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}}, Prince of Emperor {{ill|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}) | clan_patriarch = | type = {{ill|Imperial clans of Japan|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja|近江国坂田郡}} | notable_members = | descendants = }} [[File:Yamatsuteru-shrine.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山津照神社}}]] [[File:Okinaga-postoffice.jpg|thumb|300px|Okinaga Post Office, preserving the Okinaga name.]] '''Okinaga Clan''' (息長氏, Okinaga-uji) is a clan named after the "Okinaga" title. == Overview == The first mention of "Okinaga" in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} appears with {{ill|Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja|息長水依比賣}}, the daughter of {{ill|Ame no Mikage no Kami|ja|天目一箇神}}’s sixth descendant, {{ill|Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja|国忍富命}}, and the wife of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of Emperor {{ill|Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. Subsequent mentions include: 1. {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}}, a son of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}} and father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, also referred to as "Kinaga Sukune no Mikoto" in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. 2. {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, the daughter of Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto and the wife of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant burial mounds ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

(Son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}}, Prince of Emperor {{ill|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}) | clan_patriarch = | type = {{ill|Imperial clans of Japan|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja|近江国坂田郡}} | notable_members = | descendants = }} [[File:Yamatsuteru-shrine.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山津照神社}}]] [[File:Okinaga-postoffice.jpg|thumb|300px|Okinaga Post Office, preserving the Okinaga name.]] '''Okinaga Clan''' (息長氏, Okinaga-uji) is a clan named after the "Okinaga" title. == Overview == The first mention of "Okinaga" in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} appears with {{ill|Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja|息長水依比賣}}, the daughter of {{ill|Ame no Mikage no Kami|ja|天目一箇神}}’s sixth descendant, {{ill|Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja|国忍富命}}, and the wife of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of Emperor {{ill|Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. Subsequent mentions include: 1. {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}}, a son of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}} and father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, also referred to as "Kinaga Sukune no Mikoto" in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. 2. {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, the daughter of Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto and the wife of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant burial mounds ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant burial mounds ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

[[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans (Kofun Period) | clan_name = Okinaga Clan | image = | family_name = Okinaga [[Mahito]] | founder = | origin = {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}
(Son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}}, Prince of Emperor {{ill|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}) | clan_patriarch = | type = {{ill|Imperial clans of Japan|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja|近江国坂田郡}} | notable_members = | descendants = }} [[File:Yamatsuteru-shrine.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山津照神社}}]] [[File:Okinaga-postoffice.jpg|thumb|300px|Okinaga Post Office, preserving the Okinaga name.]] '''Okinaga Clan''' (息長氏, Okinaga-uji) is a clan named after the "Okinaga" title. == Overview == The first mention of "Okinaga" in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} appears with {{ill|Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja|息長水依比賣}}, the daughter of {{ill|Ame no Mikage no Kami|ja|天目一箇神}}’s sixth descendant, {{ill|Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja|国忍富命}}, and the wife of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of Emperor {{ill|Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. Subsequent mentions include: 1. {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}}, a son of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}} and father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, also referred to as "Kinaga Sukune no Mikoto" in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. 2. {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, the daughter of Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto and the wife of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant burial mounds ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:New]]

According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant burial mounds ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]]
