[[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans (Kofun Period) | clan_name = Okinaga Clan | image = | family_name = Okinaga [[Mahito]] | founder = | origin = {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}
(Son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}}, Prince of Emperor {{ill|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}) | clan_patriarch = | type = {{ill|Imperial clans of Japan|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja|近江国坂田郡}} | notable_members = | descendants = }} [[File:Yamatsuteru-shrine.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山津照神社}}]] [[File:Okinaga-postoffice.jpg|thumb|300px|Okinaga Post Office, preserving the Okinaga name.]] '''Okinaga Clan''' (息長氏, Okinaga-uji) is a clan named after the "Okinaga" title. == Overview == The first mention of "Okinaga" in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} appears with {{ill|Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja|息長水依比賣}}, the daughter of {{ill|Ame no Mikage no Kami|ja|天目一箇神}}’s sixth descendant, {{ill|Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja|国忍富命}}, and the wife of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of Emperor {{ill|Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. Subsequent mentions include: 1. {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}}, a son of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}} and father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, also referred to as "Kinaga Sukune no Mikoto" in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. 2. {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, the daughter of Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto and the wife of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant burial mounds ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]]

Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant burial mounds ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Okinaga Clan}}

[[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans (Kofun Period) | clan_name = Okinaga Clan | image = | family_name = Okinaga [[Mahito]] | founder = | origin = {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}
(Son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}}, Prince of Emperor {{ill|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}) | clan_patriarch = | type = {{ill|Imperial clans of Japan|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja|近江国坂田郡}} | notable_members = | descendants = }} [[File:Yamatsuteru-shrine.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山津照神社}}]] [[File:Okinaga-postoffice.jpg|thumb|300px|Okinaga Post Office, preserving the Okinaga name.]] '''Okinaga Clan''' (息長氏, Okinaga-uji) is a clan named after the "Okinaga" title. == Overview == The first mention of "Okinaga" in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} appears with {{ill|Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja|息長水依比賣}}, the daughter of {{ill|Ame no Mikage no Kami|ja|天目一箇神}}’s sixth descendant, {{ill|Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja|国忍富命}}, and the wife of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of Emperor {{ill|Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. Subsequent mentions include: 1. {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}}, a son of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}} and father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, also referred to as "Kinaga Sukune no Mikoto" in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. 2. {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, the daughter of Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto and the wife of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant burial mounds ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Okinaga Clan}}

Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant burial mounds ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:SuQqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Okinaga Clan}}

(Son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}}, Prince of Emperor {{ill|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}) | clan_patriarch = | type = {{ill|Imperial clans of Japan|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja|近江国坂田郡}} | notable_members = | descendants = }} [[File:Yamatsuteru-shrine.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山津照神社}}]] [[File:Okinaga-postoffice.jpg|thumb|300px|Okinaga Post Office, preserving the Okinaga name.]] '''Okinaga Clan''' (息長氏, Okinaga-uji) is a clan named after the "Okinaga" title. == Overview == The first mention of "Okinaga" in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} appears with {{ill|Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja|息長水依比賣}}, the daughter of {{ill|Ame no Mikage no Kami|ja|天目一箇神}}’s sixth descendant, {{ill|Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja|国忍富命}}, and the wife of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of Emperor {{ill|Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. Subsequent mentions include: 1. {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}}, a son of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}} and father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, also referred to as "Kinaga Sukune no Mikoto" in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. 2. {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, the daughter of Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto and the wife of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant burial mounds ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. (Son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}}, Prince of Emperor {{ill|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}) | clan_patriarch = | type = {{ill|Imperial clans of Japan|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja|近江国坂田郡}} | notable_members = | descendants = }} [[File:Yamatsuteru-shrine.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山津照神社}}]] [[File:Okinaga-postoffice.jpg|thumb|300px|Okinaga Post Office, preserving the Okinaga name.]] '''Okinaga Clan''' (息長氏, Okinaga-uji) is a clan named after the "Okinaga" title. == Overview == The first mention of "Okinaga" in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} appears with {{ill|Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja|息長水依比賣}}, the daughter of {{ill|Ame no Mikage no Kami|ja|天目一箇神}}’s sixth descendant, {{ill|Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja|国忍富命}}, and the wife of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of Emperor {{ill|Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. Subsequent mentions include: 1. {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}}, a son of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}} and father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, also referred to as "Kinaga Sukune no Mikoto" in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. 2. {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, the daughter of Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto and the wife of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant burial mounds ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:SuQqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[Category:Ngoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Okinaga Clan}}[[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Okinaga Clan}}

(Son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}}, Prince of Emperor {{ill|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}) | clan_patriarch = | type = {{ill|Imperial clans of Japan|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja|近江国坂田郡}} | notable_members = | descendants = }} [[File:Yamatsuteru-shrine.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山津照神社}}]] [[File:Okinaga-postoffice.jpg|thumb|300px|Okinaga Post Office, preserving the Okinaga name.]] '''Okinaga Clan''' (息長氏, Okinaga-uji) is a clan named after the "Okinaga" title. == Overview == The first mention of "Okinaga" in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} appears with {{ill|Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja|息長水依比賣}}, the daughter of {{ill|Ame no Mikage no Kami|ja|天目一箇神}}’s sixth descendant, {{ill|Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja|国忍富命}}, and the wife of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of Emperor {{ill|Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. Subsequent mentions include: 1. {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}}, a son of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}} and father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, also referred to as "Kinaga Sukune no Mikoto" in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. 2. {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, the daughter of Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto and the wife of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant Kofuns ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[Category:Ngoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Okinaga Clan}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Okinaga Clan}}

In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Okinaga Clan}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Okinaga Clan}}

[[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans (Kofun Period) | clan_name = Okinaga Clan | image = | family_name = Okinaga [[Mahito]] | founder = | origin = {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}
(Son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}}, Prince of Emperor {{ill|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}) | clan_patriarch = | type = {{ill|Imperial clans of Japan|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja|近江国坂田郡}} | notable_members = | descendants = }} [[File:Yamatsuteru-shrine.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山津照神社}}]] [[File:Okinaga-postoffice.jpg|thumb|300px|Okinaga Post Office, preserving the Okinaga name.]] '''Okinaga Clan''' (息長氏, Okinaga-uji) is a clan named after the "Okinaga" title. == Overview == The first mention of "Okinaga" in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} appears with {{ill|Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja|息長水依比賣}}, the daughter of {{ill|Ame no Mikage no Kami|ja|天目一箇神|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}’s sixth descendant, {{ill|Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja|国忍富命}}, and the wife of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of Emperor {{ill|Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. Subsequent mentions include: 1. {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}}, a son of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}} and father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, also referred to as "Kinaga Sukune no Mikoto" in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. 2. {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, the daughter of Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto and the wife of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant Kofuns ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Okinaga Clan}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Okinaga Clan}}

Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant Kofuns ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Okinaga Clan}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Okinaga Clan}}

[[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans (Kofun Period) | clan_name = Okinaga Clan | image = | family_name = Okinaga [[Mahito]] | founder = | origin = {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}
(Son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}}, Prince of Emperor {{ill|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}) | clan_patriarch = | type = {{ill|Imperial clans of Japan|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja|近江国坂田郡}} | notable_members = | descendants = }} [[File:Yamatsuteru-shrine.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山津照神社}}]] [[File:Okinaga-postoffice.jpg|thumb|300px|Okinaga Post Office, preserving the Okinaga name.]] '''Okinaga Clan''' (息長氏, Okinaga-uji) is a clan named after the "Okinaga" title. == Overview == The first mention of "Okinaga" in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} appears with {{ill|Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja|息長水依比賣}}, the daughter of {{ill|Ame no Mikage no Kami|ja|天目一箇神|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}’s sixth descendant, {{ill|Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja|国忍富命}}, and the wife of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of Emperor {{ill|Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. Subsequent mentions include: 1. {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}}, a son of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}} and father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, also referred to as "Kinaga Sukune no Mikoto" in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. 2. {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, the daughter of Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto and the wife of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant Kofuns ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Okinaga Clan}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Okinaga Clan}}

(Son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}}, Prince of Emperor {{ill|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}) | clan_patriarch = | type = {{ill|Imperial clans of Japan|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja|近江国坂田郡}} | notable_members = | descendants = }} [[File:Yamatsuteru-shrine.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山津照神社}}]] [[File:Okinaga-postoffice.jpg|thumb|300px|Okinaga Post Office, preserving the Okinaga name.]] '''Okinaga Clan''' (息長氏, Okinaga-uji) is a clan named after the "Okinaga" title. == Overview == The first mention of "Okinaga" in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} appears with {{ill|Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja|息長水依比賣}}, the daughter of {{ill|Ame no Mikage no Kami|ja|天目一箇神|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}’s sixth descendant, {{ill|Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja|国忍富命}}, and the wife of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of Emperor {{ill|Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. Subsequent mentions include: 1. {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}}, a son of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}} and father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, also referred to as "Kinaga Sukune no Mikoto" in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. 2. {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, the daughter of Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto and the wife of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant Kofuns ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Okinaga Clan}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Okinaga Clan}}

ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Okinaga Clan}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Okinaga Clan}}

[[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans (Kofun Period) | clan_name = Okinaga Clan | image = | family_name = Okinaga [[Mahito]] | founder = | origin = {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}
(Son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}}, Prince of Emperor {{ill|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}) | clan_patriarch = | type = {{ill|Imperial clans of Japan|ja|皇別}} | homeland = {{ill|Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja|近江国坂田郡}} | notable_members = | descendants = }} [[File:Yamatsuteru-shrine.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山津照神社}}]] [[File:Okinaga-postoffice.jpg|thumb|300px|Okinaga Post Office, preserving the Okinaga name.]] '''Okinaga Clan''' (息長氏, Okinaga-uji) is a clan named after the "Okinaga" title. == Overview == The first mention of "Okinaga" in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} appears with {{ill|Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja|息長水依比賣}}, the daughter of {{ill|Ame no Mikage no Kami|ja|天目一箇神|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}’s sixth descendant, {{ill|Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja|国忍富命}}, and the wife of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}}, a prince of Emperor {{ill|Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. Subsequent mentions include: 1. {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}}, a son of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}} and father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, also referred to as "Kinaga Sukune no Mikoto" in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. 2. {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}, the daughter of Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto and the wife of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara Period|ja|奈良時代}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant Kofuns ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} awarded the highest rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara|ja|米原市}} and {{ill|Kyotanabe|ja|京田辺市}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王}} [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Kinki clans]] [[Category:Maibara history]] [[Category:removed redirect]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Okinaga Clan}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okinaga Clan]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Okinaga Clan}} [[Category:New]]
