revision.text:
[[ja:息長氏]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[ja:息長氏]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans (Kofun Period) | clan_name = Okinaga Clan | image = | family_name = Okinaga [[Mahito]] | founder = | origin = {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofuto no Okimi|1=Daidaishaku-o|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493025}}
(Son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稚野毛二派皇子|1=Wakanuke-Futamata no Miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13264213}}, Prince of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Ōjin}}) | clan_patriarch = | type = {{ill|Imperial clans of Japan|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial clans of Japan|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} | homeland = {{ill|Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja|近江国坂田郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | notable_members = | descendants = }} [[File:Yamatsuteru-shrine.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山津照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}}]] [[File:Okinaga-postoffice.jpg|thumb|300px|Okinaga Post Office, preserving the Okinaga name.]] '''Okinaga Clan''' (息長氏, Okinaga-uji) is a clan named after the "Okinaga" title. == Overview == The first mention of "Okinaga" in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} appears with {{ill|Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja|息長水依比賣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the daughter of {{ill|Ame no Mikage no Kami|ja|天目一箇神|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}’s sixth descendant, {{ill|Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja|国忍富命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759713}}, and the wife of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦坐王|1=Hikoimasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487910}}, a prince of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Kaika}}. Subsequent mentions include: 1. {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|1=Okinaga no Sukunu no miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492327}}, a son of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦坐王|1=Hikoimasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487910}} and father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}, also referred to as "Kinaga Sukune no Mikoto" in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}. 2. {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}, the daughter of Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto and the wife of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Chūai}}. Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|1=Kuimata no Nagahiko no O|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97961931}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karahime|1=Kawara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667602}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940685}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofuto no Okimi|1=Daidaishaku-o|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493025}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稚野毛二派皇子|1=Wakanuke-Futamata no Miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13264213}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikuni Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakato Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hata Clan|1=Hata Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555454}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiji Okada|1=Seishi Okada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27165522}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara Period}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama, Shiga|en|Nagahama, Shiga|WD=Q844130|lt=Nagahama}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant Kofuns ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} awarded the highest Rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight-Grade Rank System|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubai Documents|1=Tsubai Documents|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105793781}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Tsubai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椿井文書|1=Tsubai Documents|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105793781}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara}} and {{ill|Kyōtanabe|en|Kyōtanabe|WD=Q816728|lt=Kyotanabe}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|1=Okinaga no Sukunu no miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492327}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}} {{translated page|ja|息長氏|version=103843054|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:息長氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11492326}} [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Subsequent mentions include: 1. {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|1=Okinaga no Sukunu no miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492327}}, a son of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦坐王|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487910}} and father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, also referred to as "Kinaga Sukune no Mikoto" in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. 2. {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, the daughter of Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto and the wife of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}. Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|1=Kuimata no Nagahiko no O|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97961931}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karahime|1=Kawara Shrine (Q11667602)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667602}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940685}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofuto no Okimi|1=Ohfubutsu no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493025}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稚野毛二派皇子|1=Wakanuke no Futamata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13264213}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikuni Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakato Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hata Clan|1=Q11555454|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555454}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiji Okada|1=Seishi Okada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27165522}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara Period|1=Nara period}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara|1=Maibara, Shiga}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama, Shiga|en|Nagahama, Shiga|WD=Q844130|lt=Nagahama|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant Kofuns ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} awarded the highest Rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight-Grade Rank System|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubai Documents|1=Tsubai Documents|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105793781}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Tsubai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椿井文書|1=Tsubai Documents|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105793781}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara|1=Maibara, Shiga}} and {{ill|Kyōtanabe|en|Kyōtanabe|WD=Q816728|lt=Kyotanabe|1=Kyōtanabe}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|1=Okinaga no Sukunu no miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492327}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}} {{translated page|ja|息長氏|version=103843054|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:息長氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11492326}} [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:息長氏]] [[ja:息長氏]] [[ja:息長氏]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans (Kofun Period) | clan_name = Okinaga Clan | image = | family_name = Okinaga [[Mahito]] | founder = | origin = {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofuto no Okimi|1=Ohfubutsu no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493025}}
(Son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稚野毛二派皇子|1=Wakanuke no Futamata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13264213}}, Prince of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) | clan_patriarch = | type = {{ill|Imperial clans of Japan|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial clans of Japan|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} | homeland = {{ill|Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja|近江国坂田郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | notable_members = | descendants = }} [[File:Yamatsuteru-shrine.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山津照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}}]] [[File:Okinaga-postoffice.jpg|thumb|300px|Okinaga Post Office, preserving the Okinaga name.]] '''Okinaga Clan''' (息長氏, Okinaga-uji) is a clan named after the "Okinaga" title. == Overview == The first mention of "Okinaga" in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} appears with {{ill|Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja|息長水依比賣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the daughter of {{ill|Ame no Mikage no Kami|ja|天目一箇神|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}’s sixth descendant, {{ill|Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja|国忍富命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759713}}, and the wife of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦坐王|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487910}}, a prince of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Kaika|1=Emperor Kaika}}. Subsequent mentions include: 1. {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|1=Okinaga no Sukunu no miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492327}}, a son of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦坐王|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487910}} and father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, also referred to as "Kinaga Sukune no Mikoto" in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. 2. {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, the daughter of Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto and the wife of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}. Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|1=Kuimata no Nagahiko no O|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97961931}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karahime|1=Kawara Shrine (Q11667602)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667602}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940685}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofuto no Okimi|1=Ohfubutsu no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493025}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稚野毛二派皇子|1=Wakanuke no Futamata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13264213}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikuni Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakato Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hata Clan|1=Q11555454|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555454}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiji Okada|1=Seishi Okada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27165522}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara Period|1=Nara period}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara|1=Maibara, Shiga}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama, Shiga|en|Nagahama, Shiga|WD=Q844130|lt=Nagahama|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant Kofuns ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} awarded the highest Rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight-Grade Rank System|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubai Documents|1=Tsubai Documents|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105793781}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Tsubai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椿井文書|1=Tsubai Documents|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105793781}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara|1=Maibara, Shiga}} and {{ill|Kyōtanabe|en|Kyōtanabe|WD=Q816728|lt=Kyotanabe|1=Kyōtanabe}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|1=Okinaga no Sukunu no miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492327}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}} {{translated page|ja|息長氏|version=103843054|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:息長氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11492326}} [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:History of Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofuto no Okimi|1=Ohfubutsu no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493025}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稚野毛二派皇子|1=Wakanuke no Futamata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13264213}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikuni Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakato Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hata Clan|1=Q11555454|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555454}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiji Okada|1=Seishi Okada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27165522}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara Period|1=Nara period}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara|1=Maibara, Shiga}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama, Shiga|en|Nagahama, Shiga|WD=Q844130|lt=Nagahama|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant Kofuns ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} awarded the highest Rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight-Grade Rank System|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubai Documents|1=Tsubai Documents|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105793781}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Tsubai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椿井文書|1=Tsubai Documents|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105793781}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara|1=Maibara, Shiga}} and {{ill|Kyōtanabe|en|Kyōtanabe|WD=Q816728|lt=Kyotanabe|1=Kyōtanabe}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|1=Okinaga no Sukunu no miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492327}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}} {{translated page|ja|息長氏|version=103843054|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:息長氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11492326}} [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:History of Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese Clans (Kofun Period) | clan_name = Okinaga Clan | image = | family_name = Okinaga [[Mahito]] | founder = | origin = {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofuto no Okimi|1=Ohfubutsu no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493025}}
(Son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稚野毛二派皇子|1=Wakanuke no Futamata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13264213}}, Prince of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) | clan_patriarch = | type = {{ill|Imperial clans of Japan|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial clans of Japan|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} | homeland = {{ill|Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja|近江国坂田郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata District, Ōmi Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | notable_members = | descendants = }} [[File:Yamatsuteru-shrine.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine|ja|山津照神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine|qq=}}]] [[File:Okinaga-postoffice.jpg|thumb|300px|Okinaga Post Office, preserving the Okinaga name.]] '''Okinaga Clan''' (息長氏, Okinaga-uji) is a clan named after the "Okinaga" title. == Overview == The first mention of "Okinaga" in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} appears with {{ill|Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja|息長水依比賣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Mizuyori-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the daughter of {{ill|Ame no Mikage no Kami|ja|天目一箇神|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mikage no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}’s sixth descendant, {{ill|Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja|国忍富命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninushi Tomi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759713}}, and the wife of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦坐王|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487910}}, a prince of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Kaika|1=Emperor Kaika}}. Subsequent mentions include: 1. {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|1=Okinaga no Sukunu no miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492327}}, a son of {{ill|Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosazumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦坐王|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487910}} and father of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, also referred to as "Kinaga Sukune no Mikoto" in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. 2. {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, the daughter of Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto and the wife of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}. Known as {{ill|Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} in the Kojiki and Kinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto in the Nihon Shoki. 3. {{ill|Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja|息長日子王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Hiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the brother of Empress Jingū. 4. {{ill|Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja|息長田別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Tabetsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a son of Prince {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. 5. {{ill|Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja|息長真若中比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Makana Naka Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|ja|杙俣長日子王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuyimata Nagahiko no Mikoto|1=Kuimata no Nagahiko no O|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97961931}} and a descendant of Yamato Takeru. Other notable figures include: - {{ill|Karahime|ja|香春神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karahime|1=Kawara Shrine (Q11667602)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667602}}’s deity {{ill|Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja|辛国息長大姫刀自命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkuni Okinaga Ohime Toji no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. - {{ill|Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja|額田部連息長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukata Be no Muraji Okinaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a figure in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}. - Descendants of {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備津彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命}} and {{ill|Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatowake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940685}}. According to the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, the Okinaga clan was revitalized by {{ill|Ofuto no Okimi|ja|意富富杼王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofuto no Okimi|1=Ohfubutsu no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493025}}, a son of {{ill|Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja|若野毛二俣王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakanokimi Futamata no Okimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稚野毛二派皇子|1=Wakanuke no Futamata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13264213}} and grandson of Emperor Ōjin.Hōga (2014), p. 8 The clan had strong ties to the Imperial Court, using noble titles such as "Kimi" (君) and later "Mahito" (真人), symbolizing semi-imperial status. Related clans include the {{ill|Mikuni Clan|ja|三国氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikuni Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sakata Clan|ja|坂田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sakato Clan|ja|酒人氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakato Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Hata Clan|ja|波多氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hata Clan|1=Q11555454|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555454}}. === Connection to Emperor Keitai === Scholar {{ill|Seiji Okada|ja|岡田精司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiji Okada|1=Seishi Okada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27165522}} proposed the "Emperor Keitai Okinaga origin theory," arguing for close ties between Emperor Keitai and the Okinaga clan.Ōhashi (1984), p. 122 The Kojiki describes Emperor Keitai as originating from Ōmi Province, connecting him to the Okinaga lineage.Takeda (2016), pp. 7-8 Modern interpretations affirm this connection.Nakamura (2015), p. 453 In the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara Period|1=Nara period}}, the Okinaga clan was based in Sakata District, Ōmi Province (modern {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara|1=Maibara, Shiga}} and parts of {{ill|Nagahama, Shiga|en|Nagahama, Shiga|WD=Q844130|lt=Nagahama|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}). The clan controlled key trade routes and possessed significant Kofuns ({{ill|Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja|息長古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). However, some suggest their base might have been in {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}. In the late 7th century, Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} awarded the highest Rank of "Mahito" to the Okinaga clan as part of the {{ill|Eight-Grade Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight-Grade Rank System|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}}. === Tsubai Documents and Okinaga Clan === The {{ill|Tsubai Documents|ja|椿井文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubai Documents|1=Tsubai Documents|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105793781}}, forged by {{ill|Masataka Tsubai|ja|椿井政隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masataka Tsubai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椿井文書|1=Tsubai Documents|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105793781}}, attributed Okinaga-related names to various sites in modern {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara|1=Maibara, Shiga}} and {{ill|Kyōtanabe|en|Kyōtanabe|WD=Q816728|lt=Kyotanabe|1=Kyōtanabe}}.Mabe (2020) These fabrications sought to tie the clan to local legends and geographical features. == References == === Notes === {{notelist}} === Sources === {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Ōhashi, Nobuya. *Formation of Ancient Japanese States and the Okinaga Clan.* Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1984. ISBN 4-642-02153-1 * Takeda, Tsuneyasu. *Modern Kojiki: Pocket Edition.* Gakken Plus, 2016. ISBN 978-4-05-406454-6 * Nakamura, Keishin. *Kojiki: New Edition with Modern Translation.* Kadokawa, 2015. ISBN 978-4-04-400104-9 * Hōga, Toshio. *Okinaga Clan: Forging of an Imperial Family.* Seiunsha, 2014. ISBN 978-4-434-19823-6 * Mabe, Takahiro. *Tsubai Documents: Japan’s Greatest Forgeries.* Chūōkōron-shinsha, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|ja|息長宿禰王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Sukune no Mikoto|1=Okinaga no Sukunu no miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492327}} * {{ill|Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Mate no Mikoto|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492328}} {{translated page|ja|息長氏|version=103843054|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11492326|ja|息長氏}} [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:History of Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
